methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $205M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Strand Therapeutics is a Series B-stage biotech company founded by Jake Becraft and Tasuku Kitada. The target has raised $205M to date. Our comp universe consists of eight private biotech firms at comparable stages and three public-market peers in the Biotech & Health sector.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $205M total raised proxy, implying a $390M valuation. Peer-median analysis draws on five same-stage, same-sector comparables—Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M)—yielding a median of $810M, with a range of $600M–$3.3B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 6.1x across RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) is not applicable, as target revenue is not disclosed.

Triangulation across the two applicable methods supports a valuation range of $331M–$931M, with a median of $600M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty in baseline stage-driven multiples and heterogeneity within the peer set, particularly the outsized valuations of early-stage frontier biotech firms like Eikon. Revenue visibility and clinical-stage advancement remain key drivers of valuation within this range.
